feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Consider the parents caring for adult children not in work or education | Letter
entry.content: One reader was surprised by how many people responding to Annalisa Barbieri’s advice column equate successful parenting with independenceReading the comments below Annalisa Barbieri’s latest advice column online (I wish my son wanted to spend more time with me, 28 June), I was struck by how many people equated successful parenting with raising independent adult children.But life isn’t always that straightforward. There is a largely invisible generation of midlife parents still caring for adult children who remain Neets – not in employment, education or training. Many are living with neurodivergence, severe mental illness, post-Covid syndrome or chronic ill health. Continue reading...

entry.title: Deaths in France surged 30% during hottest week of record June heatwave
entry.content: Public health authority says 2,025 excess deaths probably an underestimate and that it expects toll to rise furtherThe number of deaths recorded in France surged by nearly 30% during the hottest week of the record-breaking heatwave that scorched much of Europe last month, the public health authority has said, adding that it expected the toll to rise further.Public Health France said on Friday there had been “an increase of 29.1%, corresponding to 2,025 additional deaths compared with the previous week”. It said the figure was probably an underestimate and “mortality will rise further”. Continue reading...

entry.title: Iran Begins Khamenei’s Funeral in Show of Defiance Against the U.S.
entry.content: Authorities predict 20 million people will attend six days of ceremonies to mourn the supreme leader killed in the war with Israel and the U.S.

entry.title: Don't throw away your shot to speak to an AI Alexander Hamilton at Boston's new finance museum
entry.content: Nearly a decade after leaving its former home, the Museum of American Finance is opening its new doors to the public.

entry.title: Eye Opener: Brutal heat wave drags into July Fourth holiday weekend
entry.content: More than half of the U.S. is still experiencing intense temperatures as a brutal heat wave continues into the holiday weekend. Plus, America turns 250 years old this weekend. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

entry.title: Mission launched to rescue a falling space telescope
entry.content: Katalyst Space's LINK spacecraft is designed to capture and boost NASA's Swift observatory back to a safe altitude.

entry.title: Maps show this July 4th could be hottest ever in parts of U.S.
entry.content: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

entry.title: Dangerous heat wave impacting much of the U.S. ahead of Fourth of July celebrations
entry.content: An intense heat wave is sweeping across the East Coast as final preparations are underway for Fourth of July celebrations. Rob Marciano has a look at the forecast and its impact.

entry.title: Ukrainian woman named suspect in Monaco bombing
entry.content: The suspect was initially identified as a heavily built person appearing to be male, but Interpol now says it is looking for a 39-year-old Ukrainian woman.

entry.title: 40 killed when speeding, overcrowded bus tumbles into Pakistan ravine, officials say
entry.content: A speeding bus plunged from a highway into a rocky ravine in Pakistan, killing 40 people and injuring eight others in one of the deadliest road accidents in recent years, officials said.

entry.title: People awaiting hospital treatment to get three weeks’ notice under NHS England plans
entry.content: Move inspired by customer service provided by online retailers such as John Lewis and AmazonPeople waiting for hospital treatment will get three weeks’ notice of their next appointment under NHS plans inspired by the customer service provided by online shopping operators.Hospitals are being ordered to start telling everyone on their treatment waiting list at least three weeks before their operation, diagnostic test or meeting with a consultant. Continue reading...

entry.title: Just gave birth or are about to? Share your story and questions about costs
entry.content: The costs of having a baby in the U.S. can be hard to predict and budget for. We want to hear your stories and questions about how to navigate the system.

entry.title: This is how seriously a patient’s skin colour can affect the quality of medical care they receive | Devi Sridhar
entry.content: New reporting from the Guardian has shed further light on the ‘ethnicity pain gap’. This is what has to be done to close itRead more from the Guardian’s ethnicity pain gap seriesI always know someone is going to say something racist when they start a sentence with, “I’m not racist, but …” Nobody likes to think they would ever discriminate against someone based on the colour of their skin – and some people seem increasingly uncomfortable about acknowledging that such discrimination exists at all in the world. Yet we are now seeing a backlash from certain political groups against diversity initiatives, including from Kemi Badenoch who wants to do away with “DEI bureaucracy”, and Nigel Farage who promises to get rid of “woke” council roles such as those involved in increasing diversity, equity and inclusion.Whatever your political views, no one wants debates to be lost in emotion rather than based on the evidence. So it is helpful to come back to facts about race and how it affects people’s lives. And as new Guardian reporting on racial inequalities in pain relief reveals, when it comes to healthcare, the evidence is overwhelming: race and ethnicity are associated with differences in the quality of care people receive and, ultimately, in their health outcomes. Regardless of whether anyone is being racist, it is clear that some people receive worse healthcare because of their racial or ethnic background.Prof Devi Sridhar is chair of global public health at the University of EdinburghDo you have an opinion on the issues raised in this article? If you would like to submit a response of up to 300 words by email to be considered for publication in our letters section, please click here. Continue reading...

entry.title: Former USAID head grieves its closure while hoping for its future
entry.content: Former USAID administrator reflects on a year since USAID's shutdown and the new direction of US foreign aid.

entry.title: What's open and closed on July 3 and July 4? See which stores are operating.
entry.content: Major retail stores will be open on Friday, although some may have modified hours on Saturday, July 4.

entry.title: Summer Driving Season Is Here, and More Americans Than Ever Are Hitting the Road
entry.content: Prices at the pump are still elevated, but pressure is easing, leaving more money to spend on road-trip fun.

entry.title: Oregon ER doctors win a 'David and Goliath' battle against a national company
entry.content: In a test of a new state law, doctors in Eugene went up against a national physician staffing firm seeking to replace them. Their success is getting attention across the U.S. as other states consider similar laws.

entry.title: Tesla's Model Y L finally comes to the US with six seats and a $62,000 price tag
entry.content: Tesla has started taking orders for the Model Y Long Wheelbase in the US and Puerto Rico.

entry.title: Trump's ties to Erdogan sold him on this year's NATO summit. Turkey may win big in other ways
entry.content: U.S. President Donald Trump has berated and belittled many of the European leaders expected to attend next week’s NATO summit in Turkey but has only praise for its host, Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan

entry.title: Latin Mass, but faithful to the pope: A Minnesota church shows a different way during schism crisis
entry.content: Priests at the Church of Saint Agnes in St. Paul, Minnesota, celebrate a traditional Latin Rite Mass every Sunday, even though the practice has become a symbol of cultural and political battles among some Catholics

entry.title: Man with a Tibetan flag dies after setting himself on fire in front of UN HQ
entry.content: Officials say a man holding a Tibetan flag in front of the United Nations headquarters in New York set himself on fire and died

entry.title: Egg producers will pay $3.3 million, donate 53 million eggs to settle price-fixing claims
entry.content: The U.S. Justice Department and 17 states reached settlement agreements with three major egg producers this week to resolve allegations that the companies illegally colluded for years to raise prices, including when the cost soared to record highs last year.

entry.title: ‘A genuine wildlife emergency’: everything you need to know about the arrival of H5 bird flu in Australia
entry.content: What is the virus? Where did it come from? Are humans at risk? What does it mean for the agricultural sector and wildlife? Experts answer your questionsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA deadly strain of bird flu, known as H5 bird flu, has arrived on the Australian mainland.The federal agriculture minister, Julie Collins, confirmed in late June that a brown skua – found sick in Western Australia – had died from the H5N1 virus. The skua is a wild migratory bird. Continue reading...

entry.title: Last-minute launch problem delays satellite rescue mission for NASA
entry.content: A rush rescue mission to save a NASA space telescope remains grounded, this time because of a last-minute launch problem

entry.title: World Cup red card controversy
entry.content: One of the star players on the U.S. men's soccer team received a red card and will be sidelined for Monday’s game. According to FIFA rules, there’s no way to appeal. NBC News’ Steve Patterson has more on what his coach is saying tonight.

entry.title: Hegseth Prepared a Bombshell Plan to Cut Troops in Europe. Then It Got Nixed.
entry.content: The defense secretary wanted to adjust U.S. troop levels in Europe but pulled back after his plan was floated to the White House.

entry.title: ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips
entry.content: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipientAs Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister.Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. Continue reading...

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
The butterfly often forgets that it was once a caterpillar.
Lucky Numbers: 92, 53, 41, 87, 57, 15

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: When ironing a button-up shirt, flip it inside-out to easily iron over the button side.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: On average, 100 people choke to death on ball-point pens every year.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Ray Bradbury
entry.content: “Stuff your eyes with wonder... live as if you'd drop dead in ten seconds. See the world. It's more fantastic than any dream made or paid for in factories.” — Ray Bradbury

entry.title: 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' 4K movie review: Latest Pandora adventure a dazzling endurance test
entry.content: "Avatar: Fire and Ash -- Ultimate Collector's Edition" arrives on 4K with stunning Pandora visuals, Dolby Atmos sound, James Cameron spectacle and a massive making-of documentary.

entry.title: 125 passengers and crew hit with highly contagious stomach virus on cruise from San Francisco
entry.content: More than 120 passengers and crew members on a Princess Cruises ship that docked in San Francisco on Thursday were infected with a stomach virus while on their voyage, federal health officials said.

entry.title: Carney, Marcos deepen Canada-Philippines ties as Ottawa looks beyond U.S. trade
entry.content: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. pledged Thursday to strengthen trade, energy and security ties as Canada works to diversify its economy beyond the United States and deepen its engagement in the Indo-Pacific.

entry.title: Meta has released an app for making generative AI games
entry.content: Pocket is listed in app stores but seemingly unavailable in the US.

entry.title: Video in case against Pooh Shiesty shows him pressing for record label release, prosecutors say
entry.content: Prosecutors say a video shows rapper Pooh Shiesty pressing for his release from fellow rapper Gucci Mane's record label while an armed man blocks a door during an alleged robbery at a Texas music studio in January.

entry.title: The FBI is directing hundreds of analysts to its probe of Georgia's 2020 presidential election
entry.content: The FBI has asked its field offices across the country to dedicate more than 200 staffers to its investigation of the 2020 election in Georgia's Fulton County.

entry.title: A grand jury indicts Louisiana's attorney general in a fight over changes to the local courts
entry.content: Louisiana's Republican attorney general was indicted Thursday on criminal charges by a grand jury in New Orleans, accused of trying to intimidate local officials who fought a law enacted by GOP legislators to overhaul the local courts.

entry.title: Nevada transgender suspect accused of mass-shooting threats arraigned on 35 felony counts
entry.content: A Henderson, Nevada, transgender woman is facing dozens of felony charges after police say they found her sitting in a stolen car loaded with guns in a casino parking garage, uncovering what investigators described as a stockpile of weapons and a history of threats to carry out a mass shooting on the Las Vegas Strip.

entry.title: Cloudflare will filter out web crawlers that serve AI companies
entry.content: The hosting platform wants sites to have more control over how AI companies use their content.

entry.title: Citadel's hedge funds post broad first-half gains, top performing strategy sidesteps quant selloff
entry.content: The hedge fund firm's tactical trading fund, which combines discretionary equity investing with quantitative strategies, climbed 14.3% through the end of June.

entry.title: Massachusetts bill would let people sue ICE agents over rights violations
entry.content: Massachusetts lawmakers are questioning whether people can sue individual ICE agents in state court over alleged constitutional violations during civil immigration enforcement, a remedy that would not be limited to illegal immigrants.

entry.title: Dodgers Expect Reliever to Return From IL Before All-Star Break
entry.content: The Los Angeles Dodgers expect right-hander Evan Phillips to return from the injured list before the All-Star break.

entry.title: PFL locks down 11-bout lineup for Washington, D.C., card on July 25
entry.content: Jaws will get jacked in the nation’s capital inside of the Professional Fighters League cage at the end of this month.

entry.title: Latest MLBPA Proposal Focuses on Roster Management, Salary Protections
entry.content: The latest labor proposal centered on MLB’s roster management rules.

entry.title: How young fans and parents will tackle England's late night World Cup clash
entry.content: England's must-win match against Mexico kicks off at 01:00BST on Monday - disco naps and spoiler-free recaps may be needed.

entry.title: Former Aberdeen ace rejected English clubs to join Rangers
entry.content: Ross McCrorie return gives Rangers fresh steel under Derek McInnesRoss McCrorie has come home to Ibrox with no fuss and no fanfare, the sort of return that tends to speak for itself in Glasgow. At 28,...

entry.title: Jaylen Brown releases statement following shocking trade to Philadelphia
entry.content: Brown thanks the Boston fans and talks about earning the respect of 76ers fans.

entry.title: From Giannis Antetokounmpo to LeBron James to Jaylen Brown: A timeline of the NBA's major offseason moves
entry.content: In the span of 10 days, four Finals MVPs and a number of All-Stars changed teams.

entry.title: Olympian David Hearn charged with destruction of property at Reflecting Pool
entry.content: David Hearn is accused of ripping out a piece of sealant on the bottom of the Reflecting Pool on June 19, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said.

entry.title: 76ers' salary cap problem clear after Jaylen Brown trade, Anfernee Simons signing
entry.content: Philly is going to have to be smart about its money.

entry.title: Harvard astronomer leading White House UFO council explains what he hopes to uncover
entry.content: The White House appointed former Harvard University astronomer Avi Loeb to lead its new panel on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena, also known as UAPs and UFOs. Loeb shares his enthusiasm for his new role.

entry.title: Trump's crypto dealings and potential conflicts of interest
entry.content: New financial disclosures show President Trump made $1.4 billion on crypto in 2025. Tom Bergin, investigative financial reporter for Reuters, joins CBS News to discuss the potential conflicts of interest that comes from Mr. Trump's crypto dealings.

entry.title: Celeb planners on how MSG could become Taylor Swift's perfect wedding venue
entry.content: Two elite event designers shared their thoughts on how Taylor Swift's team might transform Madison Square Garden.

entry.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
entry.content: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.

entry.title: Takeaways from weaker than expected jobs report
entry.content: The U.S. added 57,000 jobs in June, failing to meet the expectations of economists, according to new numbers released from the Bureau of Labor Statistics. CBS News' Javier David and Natalie Brand have more.

entry.title: Travel app Hopper to pay $35M in FTC settlement over ‘unfairly’ charging hidden fees
entry.content: Hopper will pay $35 million to settle FTC allegations that it used deceptive “dark patterns” to hide fees and mislead travelers about the cost and benefits of services.

entry.title: Puyallup Tribe and First Nations in Canada hope World Cup partnerships raise cultural awareness
entry.content: A handful of Indigenous tribes are partnering with World Cup host cities to showcase their traditions and cultures

entry.title: Massachusetts city with large Cape Verdean community sets curfew ahead of World Cup match
entry.content: A Massachusetts city home to one of the nation’s largest Cape Verdean communities will impose a nighttime curfew ahead of Cape Verde’s World Cup match against Argentina

entry.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
entry.content: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.

entry.title: Why bulky laptop chargers are a relic of the past
entry.content: Laptop chargers are much easier to travel with these days, and there's one specific technology that is largely to thank.

entry.title: Man who sold land for Kushner-backed Albania resort facing criminal probe
entry.content: A man who sold land for a controversial, Jared Kushner-backed luxury development in Albania is suspected of money laundering and drug trafficking.

entry.title: Vatican Declares Schism With Traditionalist Group
entry.content: The crackdown caps decades of tension with the Society of St. Pius X over its opposition to the Catholic Church’s modernization.

entry.title: Tesla Q2 vehicle deliveries are up 25 percent over last year
entry.content: The company has been gaining traction in Europe.

entry.title: Boeing-owned Wisk Aero accused of firing manager who raised safety concerns
entry.content: A former software manager claims Wisk rushed software testing ahead of a crucial 2025 flight test.

entry.title: Tiger Woods’ hospital records will be handed over to prosecutors in Florida DUI case, judge rules
entry.content: A judge has ruled that prosecutors are allowed to review medical records related to Tiger Woods’ March vehicle crash and subsequent arrest in Florida on suspicion of driving under the influence

entry.title: Venezuelan man saved from collapsed mall eight days after earthquakes
entry.content: Security guard Hernán Alberto Gil Flores, 43, initially told rescuers not to tell his wife in case he did not surviveA 43-year-old security guard who survived last week’s devastating earthquakes in Venezuela thanks to a pocket of air in his workstation cabin has been pulled from the collapsed basement of a shopping centre amid huge cheers from international rescue teams.Hernán Alberto Gil Flores had been trapped for eight days under the rubble of the Galerías Playa Grande in the hard-hit coastal port city of La Guaira since the back-to-back quakes struck. Continue reading...

entry.title: Canadian boy dies of rabies after waking to find bat on his face
entry.content: Eleven-year-old developed symptoms 19 days after encounter in Ontario in ‘exceedingly rare’ caseDoctors in Canada say a child who awoke to find a bat resting on his nose and mouth while visiting an Ontario cottage later died of rabies, in an “exceedingly rare case” that highlights the need for better public awareness.In a report published this week in the Canadian Medical Association Journal, infectious disease physicians confirmed that the 11-year-old boy died from rabies, a fatality they said probably could have been prevented with greater awareness of how the virus is transmitted. Continue reading...

entry.title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
entry.content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom.

entry.title: Popular TV-tracking app TV Time is shutting down as company focuses on AI
entry.content: TV Time, the popular TV-tracking app, is shutting down on July 15 as parent company Whip Media pivots toward enterprise AI products.

entry.title: The Toolbelt Generation Can Now Get Federal Aid for Trade School. Here’s What to Know.
entry.content: Pell grants aren’t just for college anymore. Grant money can be tapped for workforce training.

entry.title: Lucid Motors’ CFO is out as its new CEO continues leadership shakeup
entry.content: The company announced a new slate of executive hires meant to help turn things around, as Gravity SUV sales are not taking off as expected.

entry.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword's release date has been pushed up to avoid the pre-GTA 6 scramble
entry.content: Capcom is trying to get Onimusha: Way of the Sword out the door before the fall release calendar gets really crowded.

entry.title: The Supreme Court tackled race, history and the law in fraught and reflective major rulings
entry.content: The Supreme Court has wrapped up a term with significant rulings on race and discrimination

entry.title: Fery reaches third round to keep British hopes alive
entry.content: Arthur Fery keeps British hopes alive at Wimbledon as he fights back from a set down to reach the third round, but Katie Swan is out.

entry.title: Dusty May names Cooper Flagg as top reason for joining Mavericks
entry.content: Cooper Flagg was the main reason for why Dusty May left the Michigan Wolverines and joined the Dallas Mavericks.

entry.title: Barcelona Puts World Cup Star Up For Sale And Has Interested Parties, Reports SPORT
entry.content: FC Barcelona has effectively put Jules Kounde up for sale and has reduced his price tag amid interest from Chelsea and Liverpool in the Premier League.

entry.title: Nate Bittle's 2026 NBA Summer League schedule with Toronto Raptors
entry.content: Check out when you can catch former Oregon Ducks center Nate Bittle with the Toronto Raptors during the 2026 NBA Summer League in Las Vegas.

entry.title: Mavericks found an unexpected way to flip Anthony Davis into Santi Aldama
entry.content: The Mavericks used Anthony Davis trade assets to acquire Santi Aldama from the Grizzlies in a surprising offseason move.

entry.title: Braves recall Jim Jarvis, DFA Rowdy Tellez
entry.content: The Braves shuffle their infield and shunt Tellez off the roster

entry.title: PGA Tour’s Biggest Events Deliver Ratings Gains Ahead of TV Talks
entry.content: The $20 million events are a model for the new Championship Series.

entry.title: Gulls mourn death of 'Great Escape' striker Dobson
entry.content: Paul Dobson, the Torquay United striker whose goal kept the club in the Football League in 1987, dies aged 63.

entry.title: I Make $80,000 a Year and Dave Ramsey Told Me This Is Why I’m Staying Broke
entry.content: I Make $80,000 a Year and Dave Ramsey Told Me This Is Why I’m Staying Broke

entry.title: Tesla Deliveries Soar To 480,126 In Q2; EV Giant's Stock Eyes Buy Point (Live Coverage)
entry.content: Tesla Deliveries Soar To 480,126 In Q2; EV Giant's Stock Eyes Buy Point (Live Coverage)

entry.title: Forget SpaceX: Investors Say This Is The Next Breakout AI Stock in 2026
entry.content: Forget SpaceX: Investors Say This Is The Next Breakout AI Stock in 2026

entry.title: Retail Investors Say This Is The Next SanDisk Stock Everyone’s Ignoring
entry.content: Retail Investors Say This Is The Next SanDisk Stock Everyone’s Ignoring

entry.title: HDMI 2.1 vs USB-C vs DisplayPort: Which connection is better for your monitor?
entry.content: HDMI excels for media consumption, while PC gamers prefer DisplayPort.

entry.title: Donald Trump bought a bunch of tech stock the same day he announced his AI Action Plan
entry.content: Financial disclosures show how much Trump loves tech stocks.

entry.title: Threads' ubiquitous Mr Beast spam is part of a massive crypto scam network
entry.content: Fake Mr Beast "promotions" are ubiquitous on Threads - they're also part of massive crypto scam network.

entry.title: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, far below forecasts
entry.content: June's payroll gains were much lower than the 100,000 new hires that economists had predicted.

entry.title: Breaking down U.S. win against Bosnia and Herzegovina, controversial red card and next match
entry.content: Nigel Reo-Coker, an analyst for CBS Sports Golazo, joins "CBS Mornings" to break down the United States' big win against Bosnia and Herzegovina in the World Cup, its chances against Belgium and more.

entry.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
entry.content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

entry.title: What we know about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
entry.content: Preparations are underway at Madison Square Garden in New York City for what could be Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding. But not all of Swift's fans are convinced the big day will be held at MSG. Jo Ling Kent reports on what we know about the superstar wedding.

entry.title: Russia unleashes major attack in Ukraine, kills at least 17
entry.content: Russia launched a deadly attack in Kyiv, Ukraine, following intense warnings that the ratcheted violence was imminent. CBS News' Aidan Stretch reports.

entry.title: Huge Russian strike on Ukraine's capital kills at least 17, officials say
entry.content: Russia has launched its large-scale attack on Ukraine's capital to date, killing at least 17 people and wounding scores more, authorities say.

entry.title: At least 17 killed, dozens injured in major Russian attack on Kyiv
entry.content: At least 17 people were killed and 90 injured in a Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv, officials say. Aidan Stretch has the latest.

entry.title: Dads of Camp Mystic victims push for safety changes nearly 1 year after deadly floods
entry.content: Nearly one year ago, flash flooding in Texas killed more than 130 people, including 27 campers and counselors at Camp Mystic. Jason Allen spoke with two fathers of Camp Mystic victims who are pushing for change.

entry.title: Rivian raises EV sales forecast as Q2 production ramps up
entry.content: The company now expects to ship a few thousand more vehicles by the end of 2026 than it previously expected, after launching its R2 SUV last month.

entry.title: What Germany’s Economic Overhaul Means for Its Economy
entry.content: The measures are a political gamble for a government that has seen its ratings plummet in the past year.

entry.title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
entry.content: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.
